message.content:
I need to understand how `gt rig add --adopt` works, specifically whether it supports repos outside the ~/gt/ directory (external paths). 

Search the gastown source code. The `gt` binary is likely built from a Go codebase. Check:
1. Find the source for `gt rig add` command - look in /home/skogix/.local/src/ or anywhere Go source might live
2. How does --adopt work? Does it create symlinks? Copy? Just register?
3. Is there any "port" or external path concept already implemented?
4. What happens if the rig directory is outside ~/gt/?

Search broadly - the gastown repo might be at /home/skogix/.local/src/gastown or similar. Also check /home/skogix/gt/ itself for any Go source.

content.text: I'll search for the gastown Go source code and understand how `gt rig add --adopt` works.
Great! Found it at `/home/skogix/.local/src/gastown`. Let me explore the structure and find the rig command implementation.
Perfect! Let me read the main rig command and the adopt functionality.

77:  gt rig add existing-rig --adopt`,

1â†’// Package config provides configuration types and serialization for Gas Town.
     2â†’package config
     3â†’
     4â†’import (
     5â†’	"fmt"
     6â†’	"os"
     7â†’	"os/exec"
     8â†’	"path/filepath"
     9â†’	"strings"
    10â†’	"time"
    11â†’
    12â†’	"github.com/steveyegge/gastown/internal/scheduler/capacity"
    13â†’)
    14â†’
    15â†’// TownConfig represents the main town identity (mayor/town.json).
    16â†’type TownConfig struct {
    17â†’	Type       string    `json:"type"`                  // "town"
    18â†’	Version    int       `json:"version"`               // schema version
    19â†’	Name       string    `json:"name"`                  // town identifier (internal)
    20â†’	Owner      string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`       // owner email (entity identity)
    21â†’	PublicName string    `json:"public_name,omitempty"` // public display name
    22â†’	CreatedAt  time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    23â†’}
    24â†’
    25â†’// MayorConfig represents town-level behavioral configuration (mayor/config.json).
    26â†’// This is separate from TownConfig (identity) to keep configuration concerns distinct.
    27â†’type MayorConfig struct {
    28â†’	Type            string           `json:"type"`                        // "mayor-config"
    29â†’	Version         int              `json:"version"`                     // schema version
    30â†’	Theme           *TownThemeConfig `json:"theme,omitempty"`             // global theme settings
    31â†’	Daemon          *DaemonConfig    `json:"daemon,omitempty"`            // daemon settings
    32â†’	Deacon          *DeaconConfig    `json:"deacon,omitempty"`            // deacon settings
    33â†’	DefaultCrewName string           `json:"default_crew_name,omitempty"` // default crew name for new rigs
    34â†’}
    35â†’
    36â†’// CurrentTownSettingsVersion is the current schema version for TownSettings.
    37â†’const CurrentTownSettingsVersion = 1
    38â†’
    39â†’// TownSettings represents town-level behavioral configuration (settings/config.json).
    40â†’// This contains agent configuration that applies to all rigs unless overridden.
    41â†’type TownSettings struct {
    42â†’	Type    string `json:"type"`    // "town-settings"
    43â†’	Version int    `json:"version"` // schema version
    44â†’
    45â†’	// CLITheme controls CLI output color scheme.
    46â†’	// Values: "dark", "light", "auto" (default).
    47â†’	// "auto" lets the terminal emulator's background color guide the choice.
    48â†’	// Can be overridden by GT_THEME environment variable.
    49â†’	CLITheme string `json:"cli_theme,omitempty"`
    50â†’
    51â†’	// DefaultAgent is the name of the agent preset to use by default.
    52â†’	// Can be a built-in preset ("claude", "gemini", "codex", "cursor", "auggie", "amp", "opencode", "copilot")
    53â†’	// or a custom agent name defined in settings/agents.json.
    54â†’	// Default: "claude"
    55â†’	DefaultAgent string `json:"default_agent,omitempty"`
    56â†’
    57â†’	// Agents defines custom agent configurations or overrides.
    58â†’	// Keys are agent names that can be referenced by DefaultAgent or rig settings.
    59â†’	// Values override or extend the built-in presets.
    60â†’	// Example: {"gemini": {"command": "/custom/path/to/gemini"}}
    61â†’	Agents map[string]*RuntimeConfig `json:"agents,omitempty"`
    62â†’
    63â†’	// RoleAgents maps role names to agent aliases for per-role model selection.
    64â†’	// Keys are role names: "mayor", "deacon", "witness", "refinery", "polecat", "crew".
    65â†’	// Values are agent names (built-in presets or custom agents defined in Agents).
    66â†’	// This allows cost optimization by using different models for different roles.
    67â†’	// Example: {"mayor": "claude-opus", "witness": "claude-haiku", "polecat": "claude-sonnet"}
    68â†’	RoleAgents map[string]string `json:"role_agents,omitempty"`
    69â†’
    70â†’	// AgentEmailDomain is the domain used for agent git identity emails.
    71â†’	// Agent addresses like "gastown/crew/jack" become "gastown.crew.jack@{domain}".
    72â†’	// Default: "gastown.local"
    73â†’	AgentEmailDomain string `json:"agent_email_domain,omitempty"`
    74â†’
    75â†’	// WebTimeouts configures command execution timeouts for the web dashboard.
    76â†’	WebTimeouts *WebTimeoutsConfig `json:"web_timeouts,omitempty"`
    77â†’
    78â†’	// WorkerStatus configures activity-age thresholds for worker status classification.
    79â†’	WorkerStatus *WorkerStatusConfig `json:"worker_status,omitempty"`
    80â†’
    81â†’	// FeedCurator configures event deduplication and aggregation windows.
    82â†’	FeedCurator *FeedCuratorConfig `json:"feed_curator,omitempty"`
    83â†’
    84â†’	// Convoy configures convoy behavior settings.
    85â†’	Convoy *ConvoyConfig `json:"convoy,omitempty"`
    86â†’
    87â†’	// CostTier tracks which cost tier preset was applied (informational).
    88â†’	// Actual model assignments live in RoleAgents and Agents.
    89â†’	// Values: "standard", "economy", "budget", or empty for custom configs.
    90â†’	CostTier string `json:"cost_tier,omitempty"`
    91â†’
    92â†’	// Scheduler configures the capacity scheduler for polecat dispatch.
    93â†’	Scheduler *capacity.SchedulerConfig `json:"scheduler,omitempty"`
    94â†’}
    95â†’
    96â†’// NewTownSettings creates a new TownSettings with defaults.
    97â†’func NewTownSettings() *TownSettings {
    98â†’	return &TownSettings{
    99â†’		Type:         "town-settings",
   100â†’		Version:      CurrentTownSettingsVersion,
   101â†’		DefaultAgent: "claude",
   102â†’		Agents:       make(map[string]*RuntimeConfig),
   103â†’		RoleAgents:   make(map[string]string),
   104â†’	}
   105â†’}
   106â†’
   107â†’// WebTimeoutsConfig configures command execution timeouts for the web dashboard.
   108â†’type WebTimeoutsConfig struct {
   109â†’	// CmdTimeout is the timeout for bd (beads) commands. Default: "15s".
   110â†’	CmdTimeout string `json:"cmd_timeout,omitempty"`
   111â†’	// GhCmdTimeout is the timeout for GitHub API commands. Default: "10s".
   112â†’	GhCmdTimeout string `json:"gh_cmd_timeout,omitempty"`
   113â†’	// TmuxCmdTimeout is the timeout for tmux queries. Default: "2s".
   114â†’	TmuxCmdTimeout string `json:"tmux_cmd_timeout,omitempty"`
   115â†’	// FetchTimeout is the maximum time for all dashboard data fetches. Default: "8s".
   116â†’	FetchTimeout string `json:"fetch_timeout,omitempty"`
   117â†’	// DefaultRunTimeout is the default timeout for /api/run commands. Default: "30s".
   118â†’	DefaultRunTimeout string `json:"default_run_timeout,omitempty"`
   119â†’	// MaxRunTimeout is the maximum allowed timeout for /api/run commands. Default: "60s".
   120â†’	MaxRunTimeout string `json:"max_run_timeout,omitempty"`
   121â†’}
   122â†’
   123â†’// DefaultWebTimeoutsConfig returns a WebTimeoutsConfig with sensible defaults.
   124â†’func DefaultWebTimeoutsConfig() *WebTimeoutsConfig {
   125â†’	return &WebTimeoutsConfig{
   126â†’		CmdTimeout:        "15s",
   127â†’		GhCmdTimeout:      "10s",
   128â†’		TmuxCmdTimeout:    "2s",
   129â†’		FetchTimeout:      "8s",
   130â†’		DefaultRunTimeout: "30s",
   131â†’		MaxRunTimeout:     "60s",
   132â†’	}
   133â†’}
   134â†’
   135â†’// WorkerStatusConfig configures activity-age thresholds for worker status classification.
   136â†’type WorkerStatusConfig struct {
   137â†’	// StaleThreshold is the activity age after which a worker is considered "stale".
   138â†’	// Default: "5m".
   139â†’	StaleThreshold string `json:"stale_threshold,omitempty"`
   140â†’	// StuckThreshold is the activity age after which a worker is considered "stuck".
   141â†’	// Default: "30m".
   142â†’	StuckThreshold string `json:"stuck_threshold,omitempty"`
   143â†’	// HeartbeatFreshThreshold is the max age for a Deacon heartbeat to be considered fresh.
   144â†’	// Default: "5m".
   145â†’	HeartbeatFreshThreshold string `json:"heartbeat_fresh_threshold,omitempty"`
   146â†’	// MayorActiveThreshold is the max session inactivity for the Mayor to be considered active.
   147â†’	// Default: "5m".
   148â†’	MayorActiveThreshold string `json:"mayor_active_threshold,omitempty"`
   149â†’}
   150â†’
